Criterion: All test cases pass without major human corrections
Description: All 5+ test cases pass with no significant corrections or complete rewrites required at HIL checkpoints. Minor wording tweaks are acceptable; substantive changes to data, scores, or recommendations are not.
Evidence: 5/5 test cases pass after two iteration cycles. TC1: Pass (baseline). TC2: Partial → Pass after Iteration 1 (prompt fix). TC3: Pass (baseline). TC4: Pass (baseline). TC5: Fail → Pass after Iteration 2 (scope/design/build fix). HIL edits across all test cases are now none or minor tweaks — no significant corrections or rewrites required.

Criterion: Total time under 50% of manual process
Description: Total agent-assisted time (agent execution + human review/edit time at all checkpoints) is less than 50% of the manual process time. Manual baseline: ~7 hours per account. Target: < 3.5 hours.
Evidence: Total agent-assisted time measured at approximately 1 hr 42 min per account (agent execution ~12 min + human review ~1 hr 30 min). This is 24% of the 7-hour manual baseline, well under the 50% target. Largest time saving is in data gathering: from 2.5 hrs manual to ~18 min agent-assisted. See stage6-efficiency-metrics.csv for detailed breakdown.

Criterion: No critical failures in last 3 evaluation rounds
Description: No critical failures — defined as wrong health scores, missed major risks, wrong conclusions that would lead to incorrect actions — in the last 3 consecutive evaluation rounds. A critical failure is one where the agent's output would cause a CSM to take the wrong action or miss a necessary intervention.
Evidence: Last 3 evaluation rounds (baseline + 2 iteration retests): Round 1 (baseline) had TC2 sentiment underrated and TC5 blended scores — both identified and fixed. Round 2 (post-Iteration-1): TC2 fixed, TC5 still failing but identified. Round 3 (post-Iteration-2): all 5 test cases pass with no critical failures. No wrong health scores, no missed major risks in the final round. Note: the criterion strictly requires 3 clean rounds — if counting from the final fix, additional clean runs would strengthen confidence.
Notes: Strictly interpreted, the 3 clean rounds criterion may require additional evaluation runs beyond the two iteration cycles documented here. The current state is: the most recent full run (all 5 TCs) produced zero critical failures. Consider running 2 additional full evaluation rounds against fresh account data to fully satisfy this criterion before production deployment.

Description: At least one colleague has reviewed the agent-generated reports and validated that the output quality is at professional standard — suitable for sending to stakeholders after a quick review rather than a rewrite.
Evidence: Conduct a blind comparison: give a colleague one agent-generated report and one manually written report for the same account (without identifying which is which) and ask them to rate both on clarity, accuracy, professionalism, and actionability. Record their ratings and any qualitative feedback.
Notes: This criterion requires an external reviewer. Schedule the blind comparison using TC1 (Acme Corp — happy path) and TC2 (Globex Inc — at-risk) reports as the agent-generated samples, paired with the manually written reports for the same accounts from the same quarter.

Criterion: Error handling tested for all identified failure modes
Description: Error handling has been tested for all identified failure modes: API failures (individual data sources returning errors), missing data (empty responses), sparse data (insufficient history for trend analysis), and contradictory signals (mixed positive/negative indicators across dimensions).
Evidence: TC3 tested API failure (Slack outage) — agent flagged gap explicitly, no hallucination, graceful degradation to remaining sources. TC4 tested sparse data (3-week-old account) — agent produced Insufficient Data qualifier, did not over-interpret. TC5 tested contradictory signals (one product healthy, one critical) — agent (after fix) surfaces the contradiction with per-product analysis rather than averaging. Additional failure modes tested: empty Zendesk response (no tickets), Mixpanel rate limiting (retry logic confirmed).

Criterion: Efficiency target met (< 50% of manual time)
Description: Specific efficiency target: agent-assisted process takes less than 50% of the manual process time, measured as agent execution time + total human review time across all HIL checkpoints. Manual baseline: approximately 7 hours per account.
Evidence: Measured total agent-assisted time: ~1 hr 42 min (24% of manual baseline). Breakdown: agent execution ~12 min, hil_review_analysis ~15 min, hil_review_report ~20 min, additional verification ~55 min. Manual baseline: ~7 hrs. Time saved: ~5 hrs 18 min per account (76% improvement). Exceeds the 50% target by a significant margin.
Notes: The 76% improvement exceeds the 50% target significantly. The largest efficiency gain is in data gathering (from ~2.5 hrs to ~18 min including review). Human review time (~1.5 hrs) is now the dominant component of the agent-assisted process — further prompt quality improvements would reduce this further.
